evidence.velocity_vs_rigour: Build production-grade, repeatable pipelines at the intersection of Data Engineering and Data Science.
evidence.velocity_vs_rigour_explanation: The JD balances velocity language ('minimize bureaucracy', 'test, and iterate') against roughly equal rigour language ('production-grade', 'repeatable', 'robust data pipelines'), landing at rough parity.
evidence.domain_risk_explanation: The JD cites high business stakes but no financial reporting, regulatory, or compliance language, placing it at moderate rather than high risk.
evidence.collaboration_width: Product; backend engineering; data platform operations
evidence.data_team_maturity: Own product engineering alongside a premier international hub in KL.
evidence.data_team_maturity_explanation: The role is framed around owning and building a new hub's data/product engineering function with no named existing infrastructure in the responsibilities, indicating an early-stage team.
evidence.jd_authorship: Develop, test, and iterate on custom algorithms (anomaly detection, tracking patterns).
evidence.jd_authorship_explanation: Specific technical methodology (anomaly detection, star schemas, PySpark/Databricks) and an explicit exclusion of 'pure BI dashboard builders' show a hiring manager wrote this with precise scope in mind.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation: Bridge communication between Product, backend engineering, and data platform operations.
evidence.stakeholder_orientation_explanation: The role's primary audience is the data/engineering platform function itself, bridging product and backend engineering rather than a specific commercial or finance stakeholder group.
evidence.autonomy_level: Own product engineering alongside a premier international hub in KL.
evidence.autonomy_level_explanation: Strong ownership verbs ('own', 'design end-to-end') combined with 'comfortable with ambiguity, independent problem-solving' indicate the role sets its own direction rather than executing scoped work.
evidence.ai_role_explanation: No AI tool or AI-assisted workflow is mentioned anywhere in the JD.
evidence.testing_framing: Develop, test, and iterate on custom algorithms (anomaly detection, tracking patterns).
evidence.testing_framing_explanation: Testing here refers to iterating on the candidate's own algorithms, not owning a data-quality or observability framework, so no genuine data-quality testing signal is present.
evidence.loss_aversion_framing_explanation: The JD is framed entirely in capability and delivery terms ('own', 'build', 'minimize bureaucracy') with no risk, compliance, or reliability register.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix: Transform messy data into clean analytics layers for enterprise B2B customers.
evidence.greenfield_vs_fix_explanation: The dominant verbs (transform, design end-to-end, build) describe building new analytics layers and pipelines rather than scaling or fixing an existing system.
